01:12:42	Parithosh Jayanthi:	remove submitBeaconBlock API builder-specs#162
01:13:38	cayman:	Replying to "I think this is real..."

Tts a testing / maintenance burden. Getting it right is not exactly simple. We already thought we got it right, and yet were able to cause a chain stall in testing yesterday. The tradeoff is submitting a new contract that is 99% copy of an existing audited contract. Imo not even close.
01:15:11	Dustin:	Replying to "I think this is re..."

Yeah I'm not sure I buy this narrative of delaying the fork into 2027
01:15:13	Potuz:	Replying to "I think this is real..."

Not only that, you need to change specification and then audit the new specification. The number of bugs that were introduced in the spec when we moved from builders  being validators to not was huge. This change is along the same lines. 

I completely agree that this is the right solution, but it’s not a simple change by no means
01:16:13	cayman:	Replying to "I think this is real..."

the spec change is small. it actually is a simple change.
01:16:49	Paul Harris:	Stateful is a concern I think for several ppl
01:16:53	Potuz:	Replying to "I think this is real..."

Not, really, you need to deal with different engine API, you need to add new requests, you need to remove any optimization that dealt with not having the same pub key on different slices, etc..
01:17:09	Dustin:	Replying to "Stateful is a conc..."

Yes, I disagree with adding stateful things to the APIs
01:17:51	Parithosh Jayanthi:	Replying to "I think this is real..."

That being said, is your claim that we shouldn’t care when the fork happens? If that’s the viewpoint then we should really split the fork and ship BAL/repricings, we cannot afford to hold off those changes for months when they are mostly done.
